name: Reverse Corte
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Outside Spin, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Natural Turn (4-6; or 4-6 when ending BDW), Fallaway Whisk, Back Whisk (normal; or Back whisk with turn at corner), Closed Impetus (when ending BDW), Back Lock (when ending BDW), Outside Change (at corner; or into Closed Wing), Open Impetus (when ending BDW), Outside Spin (underturned)
notes: On step 2 turn may be made on ball of RF with foot flat. Footwork would then be 1. THT; 2 Toes both feet; 3 TH(RF). Note: Other alignments (between steps 1-2) are the following, with different ending alignment: backing LOD, 1/2 L between 1-2, ending against LOD; backing LOD, 5/8 L between 1-2, ending BDW against LOD; backing DC, 1/4 to L between 1-2, ending BDC against LOD. Follower: When Man uses different alignments as above, the Lady will turn: Just over 1/4 to L between 1-2, just under 1/4 to L between 2-3; 3/8 to L between 1-2, 1/4 to L between 2-3; 1/8 to L between 1-2, 1/8 to L between 2-3.

steps.action: RF back, start to turn left
comments: Do not step sideways, straight back, use CBM

steps.action: LF closes to RF without weight, BDC against LOD
comments: Do not step sideways, straight back, use CBM
Do not pop up both toes too early. Stay down till lady gets on her RF, then rise.
Lower down through the right foot.
This is the same footwork as second half of Reverse Turn.

steps.action: RF to side, Facing Center, 1/4 turn
comments: Stay very left. Connect L shoulder down.

steps.action: LF closes to RF, DC against LOD
comments: Gracefully collect and get back (especially when extensions last extra measure.)
